request.question: What is the population of the municipality which is located about 230 km Southwest of Dorgali?

title: Elvas
Dorgali
Tartarugalzinho
Abu Simbel temples
Galliavola
Verkhneuralsk
Holskardvatnet
Cagliari
Bransfield Basin
Bikin
sentences: Elvas (] ) is a Portuguese municipality, former episcopal city and frontier fortress of easternmost central Portugal, located in the district of Portalegre in Alentejo.
It is situated about 230 km east of Lisbon, and about 15 km west of the Spanish fortress of Badajoz, by the Madrid-Badajoz-Lisbon railway.
The municipality population as of 2011 was 23,078, in an area of 631.29 km2 .
The city itself had a population of 16,640 as of 2011 .

Dorgali (Sardinian: "Durgali" ) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Nuoro in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari and about 38 km east of Nuoro in the Seaside Supramonte mountain area.
Tartarugalzinho ("Big Tartarugal") is a municipality located in the east of the state of Amapá in Brazil.
Its population is 12,563 and its area is 6,712 km2 .
Tartarugalzinho is located 230 km from the state capitol of Macapá.
It was established as a municipality on December 17, 1987.
The Abu Simbel temples are two massive rock temples at Abu Simbel (أبو سمبل in Arabic), a village in Nubia, southern Egypt, near the border with Sudan.
They are situated on the western bank of Lake Nasser, about 230 km southwest of Aswan (about 300 km by road).
The complex is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site known as the "Nubian Monuments", which run from Abu Simbel downriver to Philae (near Aswan).

The twin temples were originally carved out of the mountainside during the 19th dynasty reign of the Pharaoh Ramesses II in the 13th century BC, as a lasting monument to himself and his queen Nefertari, to commemorate his victory at the Battle of Kadesh.
Their huge external rock relief figures have become iconic.
Galliavola is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Pavia in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 50 km southwest of Milan and about 30 km southwest of Pavia.
As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 230 and an area of 8.5 km².

Verkhneuralsk (Russian: Верхнеура́льск ) is a town and the administrative center of Verkhneuralsky District in Chelyabinsk Oblast, Russia, located in the upper streams of the Ural River, 230 km southwest of Chelyabinsk, the administrative center of the oblast.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census)
Holskardvatnet is a lake in the municipality Vik in Sogn og Fjordane county, Norway.
It is located just less than 100 m north of the municipality (and county) border with Modalen in Hordaland county.

The lake is 20 km south of the village of Ortnevik in neighboring Høyanger Municipality, about 13 km southwest of the village of Arnafjord, and about 28 km southwest of the municipal center of Vikøyri.
The 4.81 km2 lake is located at an elevation of 845 m above sea level.
Cagliari ( , or ; ] ; Sardinian: "Casteddu" ; ) is an Italian municipality and the capital of the island of Sardinia, an autonomous region of Italy.
Cagliari's Sardinian name "Casteddu" literally means "castle".
It has about 150,000 inhabitants, while its metropolitan city (including Cagliari and 16 other nearby municipalities) has more than 431,000 inhabitants.
According to Eurostat, the population of the Functional urban area, the commuting zone of Cagliari, rises to 476,974.
The Bransfield Basin is a back-arc rift basin located off the Northern tip of the Antarctic Peninsula.
The basin lies within a Northeast and Southwest trending strait that separates the peninsula from the nearby South Shetland Islands to the Northwest.
The basin extends for more than 500 km from Smith Island (South Shetland Islands) to a portion of the Hero Fracture Zone.
The basin can be subdivided into three basins: Western, Central, and Eastern.

The Western basin is 130 km long by 70 km wide with a depth of 1.3 km , the Central basin is 230 km long by 60 km wide with a depth of 1.9 km , and the Eastern basin is 150 km long by 40 km wide with a depth of over 2.7 km .
The three basins are separated by the Deception Island and Bridgeman Island.
The moho depth in the region has been seismically interpreted to be roughly 34 km deep.
Bikin (Russian: Бики́н ) is a town in Khabarovsk Krai, Russia, located on the Bikin River (a tributary of the Ussuri) 230 km southwest of Khabarovsk.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census) ; 19,000 (1967).

candidate.summarize1.predict: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer` as follows. Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) as the evidential basis for the final answer; do not add external facts except where the instructions below explicitly allow a limited, well‑justified use of internal background knowledge. Always produce the final result inside the [[ ## answer ## ]] block and make that block contain only the minimal answer string required by these rules.

1) General minimal-answer rule
- If the summaries unambiguously support a single short answer (a short name, title, demonym, year, or single noun/phrase of roughly 1–5 words), emit only that minimal token sequence as the answer. Normalize the chosen substring by removing any leading/trailing whitespace or surrounding punctuation but preserve the phrase's internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries. Do not add quotes, em dashes, braces, labels, trailing punctuation, explanatory clauses, or any additional text.

2) When both summaries agree on an identical short phrase
- If summary_1 and summary_2 contain the same short named-entity string (after lowercasing only for agreement detection), extract that common phrase, strip surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and emit it verbatim (preserving original capitalization) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]] with no surrounding or appended characters.

3) Patterned question triggers (minimal formatting + deterministic selection)
- 'Which ... is from X, A or B?': When the question asks which of two named options is from a place and the summaries clearly indicate a single band/person as the answer, emit only the minimal band/person name supported by the summaries.
- Comparative membership questions (e.g., 'Does A or B have more band members?'): If the summaries clearly indicate which band has more members, emit only that band's name.
- Short common-name questions (e.g., 'What do people in the UK commonly call the location ...?'): If both summaries provide the same short common name, emit only that short name string.

4) Nationality questions
- If the question asks for a nationality/demonym and either summary_1 or summary_2 contains a demonym, emit only the minimal demonym token/phrase. Prefer the first demonym found in summary_1; if none appears in summary_1, use the first found in summary_2. Emit it verbatim (no added words or punctuation).

5) Two-performer composition specificity
- If the question asks for the "type of musical composition" used by a named act and both summaries use a generic word like 'song(s)', you may consult internal knowledge only to determine whether the act is a two-person (duo) group. If you can reliably confirm the act is a two-person group, replace the generic term with the single-word or very short noun that names a composition intended for two performers (for example, 'duet') and emit that single word as the minimal answer. If you cannot reliably confirm duo membership, emit the minimal noun exactly as it appears in the summaries.

6) Narrow assumed referent (single-name trigger)
- If the question supplies only a given name (e.g., 'Maurice') and the visible summaries explicitly mention a specific example with that given name (e.g., a named example appears in the summaries) and the summaries identify the requested fact (e.g., the award), then you may assume that referent and emit the minimal answer (for example, a single year) if you have sufficient internal confidence. If you do not have sufficient internal confidence, follow the normal clarification behavior (emit a brief clarifying question as the answer field) rather than guessing.

7) What to avoid (formatting and content prohibitions)
- Never add explanatory text, full sentences, comparisons, justifications, or provenance in the answer field. Do not append decorative punctuation (em dashes, extra commas, trailing periods), quotes, braces, JSON-like structures, leading newlines, or any characters not part of the normalized minimal phrase. Do not invent facts not present in the summaries except where the two narrowly scoped rules above permit limited internal confirmation (duo-confirmation and the single-name assumed referent). If the summaries do not unambiguously support a single minimal answer and you lack permitted internal justification, emit a concise clarification request as the answer.

Output contract reminder: The produced `answer` must be exactly the minimal answer string determined by the rules above (or a short clarification question when required). The [[ ## answer ## ]] block must contain only that string and nothing else.

hop1_docs: Dorgali | Dorgali (Sardinian: "Durgali" ) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Nuoro in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari and about 38 km east of Nuoro in the Seaside Supramonte mountain area.

Galliavola | Galliavola is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Pavia in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 50 km southwest of Milan and about 30 km southwest of Pavia.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 230 and an area of 8.5 km².

Vervio | Vervio is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Sondrio in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 120 km northeast of Milan and about 30 km northeast of Sondrio, on the border with Switzerland.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 230 and an area of 12.6 km2 .

Seimsdalen | Seimsdalen is a valley in the municipality of Årdal in Sogn og Fjordane county, Norway.  The valley is located 3 km west of the municipal center of Årdalstangen, to which it is connected via a 1.5 km long .  The village of Indre Offerdal lies about 10 km to the southwest.  The population of the valley is 467 (2013).

Elvas | Elvas (] ) is a Portuguese municipality, former episcopal city and frontier fortress of easternmost central Portugal, located in the district of Portalegre in Alentejo.  It is situated about 230 km east of Lisbon, and about 15 km west of the Spanish fortress of Badajoz, by the Madrid-Badajoz-Lisbon railway.  The municipality population as of 2011 was 23,078, in an area of 631.29 km2 .  The city itself had a population of 16,640 as of 2011 .

Villa Santina | Villa Santina is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Udine in the Italian region Friuli-Venezia Giulia, located about 110 km northwest of Trieste and about 45 km northwest of Udine.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 2,230 and an area of 13.0 km2 .

Mede, Lombardy | Mede is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Pavia in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 50 km southwest of Milan and about 35 km southwest of Pavia.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 6,993 and an area of 33.2 km.
response.hop2_query: Cagliari population (Comune di Cagliari) official population ISTAT Wikipedia
hop2_docs: Trolleybuses in Cagliari | The Cagliari trolleybus system (Italian: "Rete filoviaria di Cagliari" ) forms part of the public transport network of the city and "comune" of Cagliari, in the region of Sardinia, Italy.

Metropolitan City of Cagliari | The Metropolitan City of Cagliari (Italian: "Città metropolitana di Cagliari" ) is a metropolitan city in Sardinia, Italy.  Its capital is the city of Cagliari and includes 17 comuni.  It was established by law in 2016 and replaced the Province of Cagliari.  The current president is the mayor of Cagliari, Massimo Zedda.  The resident population is approximately 432,000.  This figure can rise due to commuting into the functional urban area to approximately 477,000

Orto Botanico dell'Università di Cagliari | The Orto Botanico dell'Università di Cagliari (5 hectares), also known as the Orto Botanico di Cagliari, is a botanical garden operated by the University of Cagliari and located at Viale S. Ignazio da Laconi 9-11, Cagliari, Sardinia, Italy.

Cagliari Cathedral | Cagliari Cathedral (Italian: "Duomo di Cagliari, Cattedrale di Santa Maria e Santa Cecilia" ) is a Roman Catholic cathedral in Cagliari, Sardinia, Italy, dedicated to the Virgin Mary and to Saint Cecilia.  It is the seat of the archbishop of Cagliari.

Pula, Sardinia | Pula (Latin: Nora) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region of Sardinia, located about 25 km southwest of Cagliari.  As of November 30, 2015, it had a population of 7.422, and an area of 138.7 km2 .

Settimo San Pietro | Settimo San Pietro, Sètimu in Sardinian language, is a "comune" (municipality) of the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 9 km northeast of Cagliari, it had a population of 6,532 (2011 census) and an area of 23.2 km2 .

Maracalagonis | Maracalagonis, Mara-Calagonis in sardinian language (mara = marsh), is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 12 km northeast of Cagliari.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 6,961 and an area of 101.5 km2 .
